text: <par>this doublet is a loan from Tukche</par>
<par>The final <latinform>-p</latinform> in these Tamangic forms in unexplained.</par>
<par>only used in 'year of the horse', otherwise Nepali loan used. irregular tone because it is probably a Tibetan loan.</par>
<par>the Gurung form implies a short vowel, contra the Tamang forms</par>

<par>Note that the initial looks like an affricate in this reconstructed form, which would then go with etymon 704 <reconstruction>*zak ⪤ *džak</reconstruction> ARM/HAND. But all the daughter dialects have an initial j=/y/, which suggests etymon 696 *yak ARM/HAND. This needs to be rectified.</par>
